rationale: The page provides detailed information on disaster management alert systems in India (SACHET, Cell Broadcast) and global initiatives (EW4All). It also defines specific environmental and risk management terms like Twister, Grey Rhino Event, and Super Typhoon, which are highly relevant for exam preparation.
cleanedText:
• Globally, over-the-counter trading in such products began in 1990s. ### Disaster Alert Systems | System | Details | | :--- | :--- | | **SACHET (India’s SMS-Based Alert System)** | • **Operationalized by:** National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA).
o NDMA headed by Prime Minister, is the apex body for Disaster Management in India established under Disaster Management Act, 2005.
• Developed by Centre of Development of Telematics **C-DOT** using the **Common Alerting Protocol (CAP)**, recommended by the International Telecommunication Union (ITU).
• Active in **36 States/UTs**, Sends geo-targeted SMS alerts during disasters like cyclones, floods, etc. | | **Cell Broadcast (Near Real-Time Emergency Alerts)** | • Being developed by C-DOT, CB alerts are broadcast directly to all mobile phones in affected area.
• Much faster than SMS—ideal for urgent disasters like tsunamis, earthquakes, lightning, etc. | | **Early Warnings for All (EW4All)** | • **Aim:** Ensure universal protection from hazardous hydrometeorological, climatological and related environmental events through life-saving multi-hazard early warning systems by 2027.
• **Launched:** At **COP27** of UNFCCC in **2022** by the UN Secretary-General.
• **Organizations:** Jointly led by **WMO, UNDRR, ITU and IFRC**.
• **Coverage:** Initially focused on 30 high-risk countries, has expanded to >100 participating nations. | #### 4 Pillars of EW4All 1. **Disaster Risk Knowledge:** Led by UN Office for Disaster Risk Reduction. 2. **Detection, Observation, Monitoring, Analysis & Forecasting:** Led by World Meteorological Organization. 3. **Warning Dissemination & Communication:** Led by International Telecommunication Union. 4. **Preparedness & Response Capabilities:** Led by International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies. --- ### 5.9. TERMS/CONCEPTS IN NEWS #### 5.9.1. TWISTER • Violently spinning (250 Miles/Hr), funnel-shaped columns of air stretching from dark thunderclouds to ground. • **Recent Occurrence:** USA • **Formation:** Develop when moist and warm air collides with dry and cold air, creating atmospheric instability and strong updrafts within thunderstorm. o Resulting wind shear causes air to spin, and if this rotation is tilted vertically and intensifies, a tornado (or twister) forms. o Majority are formed around **Gulf of Mexico**, acting as large source of moisture. #### 5.9.2. GREY RHINO EVENT • Defined as **highly probable, high impact but yet neglected** systemic disruptions. o These are **not random surprises** (like Black Swan events) but occur after clear warnings and accumulating evidence. • **Recent Occurrence:** Wayanad landslide #### 5.9.3. SUPER TYPHOON • Tropical cyclone with maximum wind speed exceeding 185 kph or more than 100 knots. o Tropical cyclones are **warm-core low pressure systems** associated with spiral inflow at bottom level and spiral outflow at top.
